About this page  Our systems have detected unusual traffic from your computer network. This page checks to see if it's really you sending the requests, and not a robot.  Why did this happen?   This page appears when Google automatically detects requests coming from your computer network which appear to be in violation of the  Terms of Service. The block will expire shortly after those requests stop. In the meantime, solving the above CAPTCHA will let you continue to use our services.  This traffic may have been sent by malicious software, a browser plug-in, or a script that sends automated requests. If you share your network connection, ask your administrator for help — a different computer using the same IP address may be responsible.  Learn more  Sometimes you may be asked to solve the CAPTCHA if you are using advanced terms that robots are known to use, or sending requests very quickly.

note:
弗吉尼亚州的一名法官本周表示，众议员德文·努内斯(Devin Nunes)不能就两个恶搞账户和一名共和党策略师的帖子起诉Twitter，裁定联邦通信法保护这家社交媒体公司免受这些帖子的责任。
纽内斯是加利福尼亚州的共和党人，他在去年对Twitter、策略师和账户所有者提起的诉讼中要求赔偿2.5亿美元，因为他说这些言论具有诽谤性。一个账户冒充努内斯先生的母亲；另一个账户冒充一头牛。
但周三，弗吉尼亚州亨里科县巡回法院(Henrico County Circuit Court)法官约翰·马歇尔(John Marshall)表示，联邦法律《通信体面法》(Communications Decency Act)第230条规定，社交媒体公司对其平台上发布的内容不承担责任，该条款在诉讼中提供了Twitter豁免权。
这项裁决对批评第230条并指责Twitter对他们有偏见的保守派来说是一个打击，Twitter和其他社交媒体公司一再否认这一点。努内斯在诉讼中辩称，Twitter对保守派有偏见，“同时放大了民主党批评者的声音”。
特朗普总统上个月发布了一项行政命令，指示联邦监管机构重新解释他们对该法律的执行情况。此前，Twitter采取行动，标记了他的一些帖子，这些帖子对邮寄投票做出了未经证实的指控。
Twitter在周四的一份声明中表示，它“坚信法院做出了正确的决定”，并表示它“对世界各地使用我们服务的每一个人，无论他们的背景或政治背景如何，都公正地执行其内容政策”。
这项裁决并没有保护Twitter用户不会因为他们的帖子而被起诉。其中一个恶搞账号@DevinNunesMa现在已经停用，另一个账号@DevinCow仍然活跃。这些账户曾在今年弹劾特朗普的过程中批评努内斯。
努内斯去年提起的诉讼指出，当时@DevinCow有1204名粉丝。在诉讼后的几天里，该账户的追随者增加到了50多万。它现在有超过73.5万名粉丝。
努内斯的诉讼称，这些恶搞账户参与了一场针对这位国会议员的“恶意诽谤运动”。它还说，策略师利兹·迈尔(Liz Mair)与这两个恶搞账户“联手”“诋毁”努内斯。这两个账户创建者的身份并不公开。
迈尔曾为“纽约时报”撰写评论文章，她在一份声明中表示，努内斯的诉讼“仍然是对第一修正案和美国核心言论自由原则的攻击”，并补充说，她正在为自己的法律辩护筹集资金。
note_en:
A Virginia judge said this week that Representative Devin Nunes could not sue Twitter over posts by two parody accounts and a Republican strategist, ruling that a federal communications law protects the social media company from being held liable for the posts.
 Mr. Nunes, a California Republican, had sought $250 million in the suit  filed last year against Twitter, the strategist and the owners of the accounts over statements he said were defamatory. One account pretended to be Mr. Nunes’s mother; the other pretended to be a cow.
 But on Wednesday, Judge John Marshall of the Henrico County Circuit Court in Virginia said  Section 230 of the Communications Decency Act, a federal law that says social media companies are not liable for content posted on their platforms, provides Twitter immunity in the suit.
 The ruling was a blow to conservatives who have criticized Section 230 and accused Twitter of bias against them, something Twitter and other social media companies have repeatedly denied. Mr. Nunes had argued in the suit that Twitter is biased against conservatives, “while amplifying the voices of his Democratic detractors.”
 President Trump  issued an executive order last month directing federal regulators to reinterpret their enforcement of that law after Twitter moved to  flag some of his posts that had made unsubstantiated claims  about mail-in voting.
 Twitter said in a statement on Thursday that it “strongly believes the court made the right decision” and that it enforces its content policies “impartially for everyone who uses our service around the world, regardless of their background or political affiliation.”
  The ruling does not protect the Twitter users from being sued over their posts. One of the parody accounts, @DevinNunesMom, is now defunct, and the other,  @DevinCow, is still active. The accounts had criticized Mr. Nunes during the impeachment proceedings against Mr. Trump this year.
 The lawsuit from Mr. Nunes last year noted at the time that @DevinCow had 1,204 followers. In the days after the suit, the account’s  following rose to over half a million. It now has more than 735,000 followers.
 Mr. Nunes’s suit alleged that the parody accounts had engaged in a “vicious defamation campaign” against the congressman. It also said the strategist, Liz Mair, “engaged in a joint effort” with the two parody accounts to “defame” Mr. Nunes. The identities of the creators of the two accounts are not publicly known.
 Ms. Mair, who has contributed opinion pieces to The New York Times, said in a statement that Mr. Nunes’s lawsuit “remains an assault on the First Amendment and the core American principle of free speech,” adding that she was raising money for her legal defense.
title: 法官称，德文·努内斯不能就模仿母牛和母亲的行为起诉推特
title_en: Devin Nunes Can’t Sue Twitter over Cow and Mom Parodies, Judge Says

note:
周四，美国疾病控制和预防中心更新并扩大了新冠肺炎的严重疾病风险名单-强调患有这种疾病的不仅仅是老年人。
最值得注意的是，疾控中心取消了65岁及以上的特定年龄门槛，适用于那些被认为有严重新冠肺炎风险的人-即那些需要住院、重症监护、通气或死于这种疾病的人。
现在，该机构强调，基于年龄的风险存在梯度。换句话说，任何年龄都有一定的风险，但这种风险随着年龄的增长而增加。50岁的人比40岁的人风险更大，60岁的人比50多岁的人风险更高。最大的风险出现在85岁及以上的人身上。
除了年龄，有某些潜在的健康状况也是增加严重疾病风险的一个明显因素。从大流行开始，公共卫生专家就把重点放在那些患有心血管疾病、糖尿病、慢性呼吸系统疾病和肥胖症的人身上。
在更新的指南中，疾控中心根据最新数据细化和扩大了导致高风险的健康状况清单。现在的名单是：
疾控中心在最新指南的公告中指出：“这些变化增加了落入高危人群的人数。”
而在名单上有多种条件的人风险更高-条件越多，风险就越大。
最后，该机构明确了可能增加严重新冠肺炎患病风险的条件。这些情况包括：哮喘、高血压、吸烟、1型糖尿病、肝病、妊娠、囊性纤维化、痴呆症等神经疾病和中风等脑血管疾病。
疾控中心指出，同样在周四发表的一项新研究表明，与患有这种疾病的非孕妇相比，患有新冠肺炎的孕妇住院的风险更高。然而，正如“纽约时报”指出的那样，怀孕和未怀孕的女性住院的门槛是不同的。研究发现，患有新冠肺炎的孕妇和非孕妇的死亡率没有差异。
疾控中心表示，了解谁面临的风险最大，对于保护他们的健康至关重要，并进一步补充道：
每个人都应该继续尽自己的一份力量来实施预防策略，比如专注于可以保持社交距离的活动，经常洗手，限制接触经常接触的表面或共享物品并对其进行消毒，当你与不住的人在一起时，特别是当你很难保持6英尺远的距离或人们在室内时，你要戴上布面。通过采取这些步骤，你可以帮助保护你自己、你所爱的人和你周围的人，包括那些最容易患重病的人。
note_en:
The US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention on Thursday updated and expanded its list of who is at risk of developing severe illness from COVID-19—emphasizing that it’s not just the elderly who suffer from the disease.
 Most noticeably, the CDC removed the specific age threshold of 65 and over for those considered at risk of severe COVID-19—that is, those requiring hospitalization, intensive care, ventilation, or those who die from the disease.
 Now, the agency emphasizes that there is  a gradient of risk based on age. In other words, there is some risk at any age, but that risk increases with age. A 50-year-old will have more risk than a 40-year-old, and a 60-year-old is at higher risk than someone in their 50s. The greatest risk is seen in those aged 85 and over.
 In addition to age, having certain underlying health conditions has also been a clear factor that increases risk of severe illness. From the outset of the pandemic, public health experts have focused on those with cardiovascular disease, diabetes, chronic respiratory disease, and obesity.
 In the updated guidance, the  CDC refined and expanded the list of health conditions that lead to high risk based on the latest data. The list now stands at:
  “These changes increase the number of people who fall into higher risk groups,” the CDC noted in its announcement of the updated guidance.
 And people who have multiple conditions on the list have even higher risks—the more conditions, the more risk.
 Last, the agency clarified conditions that  might increase a person’s risk for developing severe COVID-19. Those conditions include: asthma, high blood pressure, smoking, Type 1 diabetes, liver disease, pregnancy, cystic fibrosis, neurologic conditions such as dementia, and cerebrovascular diseases such as stroke.
 The CDC noted that  a new study, also published Thursday, suggests that pregnant women with COVID-19 are at an increased risk of hospitalization compared with nonpregnant women with the disease. However,  as The New York Times notes, the threshold for hospitalizing women is different if they’re pregnant versus not pregnant. The study found no differences in death rates between pregnant and nonpregnant women with COVID-19.
 Understanding who is most at risk is critical to protecting their health, the CDC said, adding further:
 Everyone should continue to do their part to implement prevention strategies, such as focusing on activities where social distancing can be maintained, washing your hands frequently, limiting contact with and disinfecting commonly touched surfaces or shared items, and wearing a cloth face covering when you are around people you do not live with, especially when it is difficult to stay 6 feet apart or when people are indoors. By taking these steps, you can help protect yourself, your loved ones, and others around you, including those most vulnerable to severe illness.
title: 疾控中心：这是新冠肺炎传染最重的人的最新情况
title_en: CDC: Here’s the latest on who gets the sickest from COVID-19

title: 为什么要等苹果公司呢？立即在RasPI上体验原创的ARM桌面体验
title_en: Why Wait for Apple? Try Out the Original ARM Desktop Experience Today on a RasPi

note:
Verizon周四宣布，根据该公司首席媒体官的说法，它将从Facebook和Instagram上撤下广告。
重要原因：Verizon是加入越来越多抵制这家社交网络内容审查政策的最大公司之一，这些政策包括它如何监管关于黑人生命也是抗议活动的错误信息，以及处理特朗普总统发布的内容。
威瑞森的首席媒体官约翰·尼蒂在一份声明中告诉Axios，他们的意思是：“我们有严格的内容政策，当这些政策被违反时，我们是零容忍的，我们会采取行动。”在一份声明中，Verizon的首席媒体官约翰·尼蒂(John Nitti)告诉Axios。“我们将暂停我们的广告，直到Facebook能够创造出一个可以接受的解决方案，让我们感到舒服，并且与我们与YouTube和其他合作伙伴的做法一致.”
快速赶上：包括反诽谤联盟(Anti-Defamation League)和全国有色人种协进会(NAACP)在内的六个民权组织上周开始敦促营销人员停止购买Facebook广告，并使用#stophatefor Profit的标签。
我们的思想泡沫，来自Axios&39；Ina Fry：虽然Verizon最出名的是提供手机和有线互联网服务，但它拥有美国在线(AOL)和雅虎(Yahoo)，目标是与Facebook争夺数字广告收入。
note_en:
Verizon announced on Thursday it will pull advertisements from Facebook and Instagram, per the company's chief media officer.
 Why it matters: Verizon is one of the largest companies to join a growing boycott of the social network over its content moderation policies, including how it polices  misinformation about Black Lives Matter protests, and  handles content posted  by President Trump.
 What they're saying: “We have strict content policies in place and have zero tolerance when they are breached, we take action,” Verizon’s Chief Media Officer John Nitti told Axios in a statement. “We’re pausing our advertising until Facebook can create an acceptable solution that makes us comfortable and is consistent with what we’ve done with YouTube and other partners.”
  Catch up quick: Six civil rights groups, including the Anti-Defamation League and NAACP, began urging marketers last week to stop buying Facebook ads, using the hashtag  #stophateforprofit.
  Our thought bubble, via Axios' Ina Fried: While best known for providing cell and wired internet service, Verizon owns AOL and Yahoo and aims to compete with Facebook for digital ad dollars.
title: Verizon从Facebook和Instagram上撤下广告
title_en: Verizon Pulls Ads from Facebook and Instagram

note:
美国全国广播公司(NBC)的孔雀流媒体服务将于下月推出，首次公开发行的明珠无疑是根据奥尔德斯·赫胥黎(Aldous Huxley)1932年经典反乌托邦小说改编的雄心勃勃的“美丽新世界”(The Brave New World)。这部“孔雀”原创系列的完整预告片终于出炉了，奥尔登·埃伦瑞奇(Alden Ehrenreich)饰演赫胥黎笔下的反英雄--野人约翰(John The Savage)，当他被推入一个乌托邦社会时，他发现自己很难适应。
正如我们在四月份报道的那样，“美丽的新世界”的故事背景设定在2540年，在世界之都伦敦，人们出生在人造子宫里，通过睡眠学习接受灌输，以适应他们指定的预先确定的种姓。公民经常服用一种名为SOMA的药物(部分是抗抑郁剂，部分是迷幻剂)，以保持他们的温顺，并帮助他们遵守严格的社会法律。乱交受到鼓励，但怀孕(对女性而言)是令人羞愧的原因。不用说，艺术和科学(尽管程度较小)都受到怀疑。
西欧常驻世界管理员穆斯塔法·蒙德告诉这部小说的反英雄主人公野人约翰，纯科学领域的每一项发现都有潜在的颠覆性。科学是危险的，我们必须小心翼翼地用铁链锁住它，给它戴上嘴巴。
约翰是一位高级政府官员的私生子，在萨维奇保留地出生和长大，那里的人仍然在生育，年龄自然，通常代表着世界国家精心控制的理想的对立面。他唯一受过教育的是莎士比亚全集。(小说的标题引用了米兰达在“暴风雨”中的一句话。)。当约翰和他的母亲琳达找到回到世界之州的路时，他最初成为了塞莱布雷的事业，但却在努力适应新的社会习俗。更具体地说，他爱上了一位名叫列尼娜·克劳恩的年轻女子，但他无法应对她的滥交和性行为。他最终将自己与社会隔离开来，希望洗清自己的罪孽。事情对任何人来说都不会有好的结局。
在4月的预告片中，我们遇到了Beta-Plus Lenina(杰西卡·布朗·芬德利Jessica Brown Findlay饰)，丢下一张索玛的标签，与各种潜在的性伴侣狂欢，偶尔的情妇伯纳德·马克思(亨利·劳埃德饰)嫉妒地看着。我们还看到了Savageland，琳达(黛米·摩尔饰)敦促她的儿子约翰(Ehrenreich饰)和她一起回到新伦敦。我们还看到了变性的威权主义者穆斯塔法·蒙德(Nina Sosanya)向一群追随者讲授提问或欢迎新想法的危险：病毒进入细胞。故事就是这样开始的。同样换了性别：伯纳德的朋友、情绪工程学院教授赫尔姆霍兹·沃森(Helmholtz Watson)现在是汉娜·约翰-卡门(Hannah John-Kamen)饰演的威廉敏娜·赫尔姆·沃森(Wilhelmina Helm&34；Watson)。
新的预告片为我们提供了更多的细节和即将到来的事情的暗示。它以列尼娜略微提到索玛开场，而伯纳德的声音则向我们介绍了所谓的乌托邦--新伦敦。每个人都很快乐，每个人都知道自己的位置，没有饥饿、暴力或痛苦--你明白的。约翰的母亲琳达告诉他，我一直想为你做这件事。剩下的大部分预告片都是关于约翰在努力适应新伦敦时的反应。他被告知，你所要做的就是连接，这似乎涉及到一种特殊的隐形眼镜，它会连接到你的眼球上。
每个人都时时刻刻都是相互联系的，这意味着没有人是真正孤独的，这似乎一点也不适合约翰。他在场但不在现场，观察但不吸引人，而且他似乎不喜欢他所看到的。你知道，我一直在关注你们这些人，他一度说。如果这个地方如此完美，为什么一切都颠倒了呢？我们看到伯纳德在这个原本很理想的城市里靠在一具身体上，所以约翰说得有道理。唯一的亮点是莱尼娜，她发现自己被野蛮人所吸引，而他也被她所吸引。约翰显然对新伦敦产生了颠覆性的影响，这一点并不是没有被当权者忽视的。蒙德说：我们需要在这件事走得太远之前阻止它。
早在4月份，我就写道，虽然孔雀进入流媒体领域相对较晚，但这部电视剧看起来可能会成为这项羽翼未丰的服务的赢家。我坚持这种印象。这部电视剧在视觉上引人注目，演员阵容一流，节目制作人大卫·维纳看起来像是富有想象力地更新了赫胥黎21世纪小说的技术细节。“美丽的新世界”将于2020年7月15日首次亮相，作为孔雀流媒体服务推出的一部分。
note_en:
NBC's  Peacock streaming service launches next month, and the jewel in the crown of its initial offerings is undoubtedly   Brave New World, an ambitious adaptation of Aldous Huxley's classic 1932 dystopian novel. The full trailer for this Peacock original series is finally here, starring Alden Ehrenreich as Huxley's antihero, John the Savage, who finds himself struggling to adapt when he is thrust into a utopian society.
  As  we reported in April,  Brave New World is set in the year 2540, in the World State city of London, where people are born in artificial wombs and indoctrinated through "sleep-learning" to fit into their assigned predetermined caste. Citizens regularly consume a drug called soma (part anti-depressant, part hallucinogen) to keep them docile and help them conform to strict social laws. Promiscuity is encouraged, but pregnancy (for women) is a cause for shame. Needless to say, both art and science (albeit to a lesser extent) are viewed with suspicion.
 "Every discovery in pure science is potentially subversive," Resident World Controller of Western Europe Mustapha Mond tells the novel's antihero protagonist, John the Savage. "Science is dangerous; we have to keep it most carefully chained and muzzled."
 John is the illegitimate son of a high-level government official, born and raised on the Savage Reservation, where people still give birth, age naturally, and generally represent the opposite of the World State's carefully controlled ideals. His only education has been the complete works of Shakespeare. (The novel's title references a line by Miranda in  The Tempest.) When John and his mother, Linda, find their way back to the World State, he initially becomes a cause célèbre but struggles to adapt to the new social mores. Specifically, he falls in love with a young woman named Lenina Crowne but can't deal with her promiscuity and sexual forwardness. He ultimately isolates himself from society in hopes of purging himself of "sin." Things don't end well for anyone.
 In the April teaser, we met Beta-Plus Lenina (Jessica Brown Findlay), dropping a tab of soma and partying with various potential sexual partners, as occasional paramour Bernard Marx (Henry Lloyd) looked on with jealousy. And we got a glimpse of Savageland, as Linda (Demi Moore) urged her son John (Ehrenreich) to return with her to New London. We also saw gender-swapped authoritarian Mustapha Mond (Nina Sosanya) lecturing a group of acolytes about the danger of asking questions or welcoming new ideas: "A virus enters a cell. That's how it begins." Also gender-swapped: Bernard's friend Helmholtz Watson, a professor at the College of Emotional Engineering, is now Wilhelmina "Helm" Watson, played by Hannah John-Kamen.
    The new trailer gives us a few more details and hints of things to come. It opens with Lenina dropping a bit of soma, while Bernard's voice introduces us to the supposed utopia that is New London. Everyone is happy, everyone knows their place, there's no hunger or violence or pain—you get the idea. "I've always wanted that for you," John's mother Linda tells him. Much of the remaining trailer is devoted to John's reaction as he struggles to adapt to New London. "All you have to do is connect," he is told, which seems to involve a special kind of contact lens that wires into your eyeball.
 Everyone is connected, all the time, which means no one is ever truly alone, which doesn't seem to suit John at all. He's present but apart, observing but not engaging, and he doesn't seem to like what he sees. "You know I've been watching you people," he says at one point. "If this place is so perfect, why is everything upside down?" We see Bernard leaning over a dead body in this otherwise ideal city, so John has a point. The only bright spot is Lenina, who finds herself drawn to the savage, and he to her. John is clearly having a disruptive influence on New London, something that does not go unnoticed by those in power. "We need to stop this before it goes too far," Mond says.
 Back in April, I wrote that "while Peacock is relatively late to the streaming scene, the series looks like it could be a winner for the fledgling service." I stand by that impression. The series is visually striking and has a stellar cast, and showrunner David Wiener looks like he has imaginatively updated the technological details of Huxley's novel for the 21st century.  Brave New World debuts on July 15, 2020 as part of the launch of the Peacock streaming service.
title: 在“美丽新世界”的预告片中，除了野人约翰，每个人都知道自己的位置。
title_en: In trailer for Brave New World, everyone but John the Savage knows their place

note: 嘿，库比蒂诺的朋友们！当你在Safari中输入Realty.com(我的公司)，然后按GO，就会进入Realtor.com(我的竞争对手)。有人能帮我个忙把这个修好吗？看起来不那么合法。顺便说一句。关闭Safari建议可以解决问题(但最终用户不会这样做)。谢谢！
note_en: Hey Cupertino Folks! When you type  Realty.com (my company) into Safari and press go it takes you to  Realtor.com (my competition). Can someone do me a solid and fix this? Seems not that legal. btw... turning off Safari Suggestions resolves the issue (but end users don't do that). Thx!
title: 为什么Safari会将确切的域条目重定向到他们认为您想要的位置？
title_en: Why does Safari redirect exact domain entries to what they think you want?

note:
现在大家都知道，虽然新冠肺炎连很年轻的人都能打，但年纪大的人面临的风险最大。在受重创的富裕国家，约60%的死于该疾病的人年龄在80岁及以上。然而，美国是个例外。疾病控制中心(CDC)6月16日发布的数据显示，该国的死亡人数明显偏年轻。在那里，80多岁的人占新冠肺炎死亡人数的不到一半，与此同时，40多岁、50多岁和60多岁的人占死亡人数的比例要大得多。美国新冠肺炎患者的中位数是48岁，意大利是63岁。
为什么美国如此离群索居？部分原因当然在于这样一个事实，即美国的人口比欧洲年轻。美国的平均年龄只有38岁；意大利是45岁。另一个原因可能是，美国中年人可能比他们的欧洲同龄人不健康，例如，因为他们往往更肥胖。
不管是什么原因，美国新冠肺炎受害者的相对年轻意味着冠状病毒正在夺走美国人更多宝贵的生命。一组苏格兰研究人员最近的一项研究将受害者的潜在健康状况考虑在内，估计了新冠肺炎按年龄损失的寿命。研究发现，在意大利，死于50多岁、60多岁和70多岁的人通常分别少活了30岁、21岁和12岁。那些80多岁的人平均损失了5年。
将这些估计应用于美国疫情的受害者，粗略的计算表明，到目前为止，新冠肺炎已经将美国受害者的寿命平均缩短了11年，而受影响最严重的欧洲国家大约为9年。美国可能还没有达到像英国、西班牙和意大利那样的新冠肺炎死亡率。但在美国的传染远未得到遏制。安东尼·福奇(Anthony Fuci)是负责美国应对的专家之一，他警告说，该国部分地区的新感染人数“令人不安地激增”。当考虑到年龄因素时，美国人更有理由感到不安。
note_en:
IT IS NOW well-known that, although covid-19 can strike even the very young, older folk face the greatest risk. In hard-hit rich countries, about 60% of all deaths from the disease are among people aged 80 and over. America, however, is an exception.  Data released on June 16th by the Centres for Disease Control (CDC) show that the country’s death toll skews significantly younger. There, people in their 80s account for less than half of all covid-19 deaths; people in their 40s, 50s and 60s, meanwhile, account for a significantly larger share of those who die. The median covid-19 sufferer in America is a 48-year-old; in Italy it is a 63-year-old.
 Why is America such an outlier? Part of the explanation surely lies in the fact that America has a younger population than Europe does. America’s median age is just 38; Italy’s is 45. Another reason, perhaps, is that middle-aged Americans may be less healthy than their European peers, eg, because they tend to be more obese.
  Whatever the cause, the relative youthfulness of America’s covid-19 victims means that the coronavirus is robbing Americans of more years of precious life. A recent study by a group of Scottish researchers estimated the number of years of life lost to covid-19 by age, taking account of the victims’ underlying health conditions. It found that in Italy, people who died in their 50s, 60s, and 70s typically lost 30, 21, and 12 years, respectively. Those in their 80s lost five years, on average.
 Applying these estimates to the victims of America’s outbreak, a back-of-the-envelope calculation suggests that covid-19 has so far shortened the lives of its American victims by 11 years, on average, compared with about nine years in the hardest-hit European countries. America may not yet have reached the same rate of covid-19 deaths as the likes of Britain, Spain and Italy. But the contagion in America is far from contained. Anthony Fauci, one of the experts in charge of America’s response, has warned of a “disturbing surge” in new infections in parts of the country. And when the age profile is factored in, there is even more reason for Americans to be disturbed.
title: 当新冠肺炎的死亡按年龄进行分析时，美国是一个异常值。
title_en: When Covid-19 deaths are analysed by age, America is an outlier

note:
还记得查看器(Viewfinder)吗？马特·斯塔克(Matt Stark)正在开发的那款耗尽小脑精力的独立游戏，其特点是一些酷毙了的递归效果。经过一段时间的拖延，马特仍然在修补它，并添加新的机制--包括上面这个：
在Viewfinder中，您将能够从墙上捕捉绘画，并将它们变成3D游戏世界中的真实对象。(这里是爱德华·霍珀(Edward Hopper)1929年的油画。)下面是斯塔克是如何做到这一点的：
Blender有一个工具，可以让你使用相机投影生成UV，马特解释说，这就是我如何将绘画纹理应用到网格上的。
最棘手的是做一个网格来适应这幅画--起初我以为这很容易，因为这幅画是一堆几何物体，但后来发现这幅画的透视在很多地方都有点摇晃。&#34；(这就是为你准备的Hopper。)。
捕捉方法类似于他在这里所描述的：&#34；当玩家拍摄照片时，我复制环境，将其灰度化并将网格切片以移除照片之外的任何东西。当他们把它放到这个世界上时，我会把环境中的网格切成一个洞，用来放照片。
当Viewfinder最终开始阅读播放测试时，请关注Matt以获取更新，不过我肯定也会在这里提到这一点。
note_en:
Remember Viewfinder,  that cerebellum-straining indie game in development  by Matt Stark featuring  some insanely cool recursion effects? After some delay , Matt is still tinkering away it and adding new mechanics -- including this one above:
  In Viewfinder, you will be able to "capture" paintings from off a wall, and turn them into real objects in the 3D game world. (A 1929 Edward Hopper painting, in this case.) Here's roughly how Mr. Stark does this:
    "Blender has a tool that lets you generate UVs using a camera projection," Matt explains, "so that's how I got the painting texture applied to the mesh.
  "The tricky bit was making a mesh to fit the painting -- at first I thought it would be easy cause the painting's a bunch of geometric objects, but it turns out the painting's perspective is a little wonky in a bunch of places." (That's Hopper for you.)
  The capture method is  similar to how he described it here: "When the player takes a photo I duplicate the environment, make it greyscale and slice the meshes to remove anything outside the photo. When they place it into the world I slice the environment's meshes to make a hole for the photo."
  Follow Matt for updates for when Viewfinder will finally be reading for playtesting, though I will surely mention it here too.
title: 独立游戏正在开发中，具有很酷的“将2D绘画变成3D现实”的功能
title_en: Indie game in development with cool “turn 2D paintings into 3D reality” feature

note:
苹果已经收购了设备管理初创公司Fleetsmith。作为收购的一部分，将加入苹果的技术和人员可能有助于苹果扩大设备注册，并引入更好的方式在组织内设置iPad和Mac等新设备。
Fleetsmith向客户(以及苹果公司)提出的建议似乎完全契合了我们这个时代：该公司为组织提供了一种方法，让远程员工(或其他不在中心办公室的员工)配备设备，一旦这些设备重新启动，就会自动注册和设置，供企业使用。在那之后，Fleetsmith会自动确保设备获得所需的软件更新。它还为IT经理提供了管理车队的仪表盘。
如果你使用过JAMF，一个更广泛的竞争对手，你就会大致了解。但Fleetsmith已经特别关注苹果设备，它有一种类似苹果的设计敏感性，而且对苹果来说，这可能是一个比JAMF便宜得多的选择。JAMF似乎走上了一条不同的道路，计划进行30亿美元的IPO。
不过，说到钱，苹果和Fleetsmith都没有透露收购价格。不过，Fleetsmith确实发表了一篇关于此次收购的博客文章。这里是它说的一些内容：
我们创建了Fleetsmith，以平衡IT的管理和安全需求与用户喜爱的Mac、iPad和iPhone体验。我们为我们建立的才华横溢的团队感到自豪，我们一直忠于我们的使命：让每个人都能获得强大、安全的苹果车队管理。
我们很高兴能加入苹果公司。我们共同的价值观是以客户为中心，而不牺牲隐私和安全，这意味着我们可以真正履行我们的使命，将Fleetsmith交付给世界各地各种规模的企业和机构。
该帖子还指出，Fleetsmith将继续为新客户和现有客户提供服务，因此这不是纯粹的知识产权或人力资本收购。据我们所知，它似乎会继续运营。
苹果不太可能在不久的将来停止与JAMF的合作，但通过收购Fleetsmith，库比蒂诺显然对自己的内部设备管理战略有了一些特殊的计划。我们只能等着看它们是什么了。
note_en:
Apple has acquired device-management startup Fleetsmith. The technology and personnel that will join Apple as part of the acquisition could help Apple expand upon device enrollment and introduce better ways to set up new devices like iPads and Macs within organizations.
 Fleetsmith's proposition to customers (and Apple) seems perfectly tailored to our times: the company offers a way for organizations to equip remote workers' (or workers otherwise not located in the central office) devices and have those devices automatically registered and set up for enterprise use as soon as they're first turned on. After that, Fleetsmith automatically ensures devices get needed software updates. It also provides IT managers with a dashboard for managing the fleet.
 If you've used Jamf, a more widespread competitor, you get the general idea. But Fleetsmith already had a special focus on Apple devices, it has an Apple-like design sensibility, and it was likely a much cheaper option for Apple than Jamf, to boot. Jamf appears to be on a different path, with a $3 billion  IPO planned.
 Speaking of money, though, neither Apple nor Fleetsmith has revealed the purchase price. Fleetsmith did publish a  blog post about the acquisition, though. Here's some of what it says:
 We started Fleetsmith to balance the management and security needs of IT with the experience users love about Mac, iPad, and iPhone. We’re proud of the incredibly talented team we’ve built, and that we’ve stayed true to our mission: to make powerful, secure Apple fleet management available to everyone.
 We’re thrilled to join Apple. Our shared values of putting the customer at the center of everything we do without sacrificing privacy and security, means we can truly meet our mission, delivering Fleetsmith to businesses and institutions of all sizes, around the world.
 The post also notes that Fleetsmith will continue to serve both new and existing customers, so this is not an acquisition purely of intellectual property or human capital. It seems like Fleetsmith as we know it will continue operating.
 It's unlikely that Apple will stop working with Jamf in the near future, but with this acquisition of Fleetsmith, Cupertino clearly has some special plans for its own in-house device management strategy. We'll just have to wait and see what they are.
title: 苹果进行另一笔收购：IT初创公司Fleetsmith
title_en: Apple makes another acquisition: IT startup Fleetsmith

note:
尽管苹果公司负责全球营销的高级副总裁菲尔·席勒否认公司正在做出任何改变，但一项新的政策将允许开发者挑战他们被该平台拒绝的规则，这表明改变正在酝酿之中。
根据其自己的数据，App Store在2019年促成了超过5000亿美元的电子商务交易。作为参考，联邦政府已经向美国企业发放了约5290亿美元的贷款，作为Paycheck保护计划的一部分。
鉴于其庞大的覆盖范围，现在是苹果改变条款的时候了吗？它是否会允许其收入份额温和地进入那个美好的夜晚，或者它是否有足够的资源来遏制新的立法，并安抚日益直言不讳的软件开发人员社区？为了研究这些问题，TechCrunch的四名员工加入了进来：
苹果开始意识到，其培养应用程序经济的简单化和家长式做法可能弊大于利。情况并不总是这样：在早先的日子里，仅仅是为了获得参与其快速扩张的市场的特权，就值得付钱给苹果。
但数字经济已经摆脱了以前推动增长的条件：一开始是新奇的，然后是由社交媒体增压的蓬勃发展的广告市场。钟摆正在向更传统的支付模式摇摆：对严肃的服务进行一次性和订阅支付。想象一下！
再加上移动平台的出现，不仅是简单的消费和通信工具，而且是严肃的工作和生产力的工具，赌注已经上升。人们开始问，苹果向任何想要使用其平台的人寻求租金，到底提供了什么价值？
苹果当然应该为它的麻烦付出一些代价，但它只占一家公司收入的四分之一多一点？对于一款99美分的应用程序来说，一对开发人员只是很高兴地卖出了几千份，现在看起来有点过分，但现在看起来确实是敲诈勒索。
苹果处于强势地位，可能会继续撼动整个行业，但它担心在努力使其平台真正有利于生产率的过程中失去合作伙伴。这个市场更大，更复杂，有跨平台和跨设备的复杂性，其中App Store和iOS可能只是一小部分-但需要令人难以置信的超大份额。
它会松开把手，但不着急。如果过于宽容，让其新规则被玩弄并仓促修改，那将是一种代价高昂的侮辱。允许开发者反击他们不喜欢的规则给了苹果很多东西可用，但没有承诺。毫无疑问，大玩家将获得更大的话语权，App Store的新常态将反映出金钱利益之间的缓和，而不是苹果慷慨的改变主意。
note_en:
Although  Apple’s   Senior Vice President of worldwide marketing Phil Schiller  denied the company was making any changes, a new policy will let developers  challenge the very rules by which they were rejected from the platform, which suggests that change is in the air.
 According to its own numbers, the App Store facilitated more than $500 billion in e-commerce transactions in 2019. For reference, the  federal government has given out about $529 billion in loans to U.S. businesses as part of the Paycheck Protection Program.
 Given its massive reach, is it time for Apple to change its terms? Will it allow its revenue share to go gently into that good night, or does it have enough resources to keep new legislation at bay and mollify an increasingly vocal community of software developers? To examine these questions, four TechCrunch staffers weighed in:
   Apple is starting to see that its simplistic and paternalistic approach to cultivating the app economy may be doing more harm than good. That wasn’t always the case: In earlier days it was worth paying Apple simply for the privilege of taking part in its fast-expanding marketplace.
 But the digital economy has moved on from the conditions that drove growth before: Novelty at first, then a burgeoning ad market supercharged by social media. The pendulum is swinging back to more traditional modes of payment: one-time and subscription payments for no-nonsense services. Imagine that!
 Combined with the emergence of mobile platforms not just as tools for simple consumption and communication but for serious work and productivity, the stakes have risen. People have started asking, what value is Apple really providing in return for the rent it seeks from anyone who wants to use its platform?
 Surely Apple is due something for its troubles, but just over a quarter of a company’s revenue? What seemed merely excessive for a 99-cent app that a pair of developers were just happy to sell a few thousand copies of now seems positively extortionate.
 Apple is in a position of strength and could continue shaking down the industry, but it is wary of losing partners in the effort to make its platform truly conducive to productivity. The market is larger and more complicated, with cross-platform and cross-device complications of which the App Store and iOS may only be a small part — but demanding an incredibly outsized share.
 It will loosen the grip, but there’s no hurry. It would be a costly indignity to be too permissive and have its new rules be gamed and hastily revised. Allowing developers to push back on rules they don’t like gives Apple a lot to work with but no commitment. Big players will get a big voice, no doubt, and the new normal for the App Store will reflect a detente between moneyed interests, not a generous change of heart by Apple.
title: 四种观点：苹果会削减App Store费用吗？
title_en: Four perspectives: Will Apple trim App Store fees?

note:
免费更新将于7月3日到来，这标志着定于夏季发布的两波新内容中的第一波(无论如何，对北半球的玩家来说是如此)。这一更新邀请玩家跳入海洋，四处游泳，收集海葵、海星、鳗鱼和其他海洋动物，然后这些动物可以捐赠给他们的博物馆藏品。神秘的后半部分更新定于8月初发布。
[公告]进入#AnimalCrossing：New Horizons免费夏季更新-第一波，7/3到来，为这个夏天降温吧！穿上潜水服潜水和在海洋中游泳，甚至可以结识新角色！请继续关注计划于8月初发布的第2波的相关信息。pic.twitter.com/cYd86R7g6D。
游戏还将增加另一个新角色，帕斯卡，一只可以为你提供新食谱的海獭。任何玩过动物穿越游戏的人都会认出帕斯卡是一个冷静的人，他在漫不经心地踩水的同时，也同样散发着冰冷的智慧珍珠。
对于一项围绕着熟悉的循环-收集水果、拔草、摇晃树木以找到漂亮的客厅家具-的游戏来说，游泳和跳水的增加实际上是一个相当大的变化。对于那些在大流行初期严厉打击“新地平线”的人来说，这可能是一个很好的理由，因为他们已经没有什么可以做的了，可以重新审视这个游戏。看看任天堂在New Horizons商店里还有什么会很有趣，因为这是游戏时代第一款期待发布后大量可下载内容的游戏，已经在路线图上规划了出来。
如果像我一样，你的妻子不小心弄坏了你的一个开关控制器，而你已经三个星期没有玩了，这也是一个完美的时间，可以在你的村民中随意漫步，同时表现得好像没有经过任何时间。Lolly，如果你正在读这篇文章，我想让你知道他们是延期订购的，这不会改变我们之间的任何事情。真的。
note_en:
The free update will arrive on July 3, marking the first of two waves of new content due out in the summer season (for players in the Northern Hemisphere, anyway!). The update invites players to plunge into the ocean and swim around to collect anemones, starfish, eels and other sea-faring creatures, which can then be donated to their museum collection. The mysterious second half of the update is due out in early August.
 [Announcement] Cool off this summer by diving into the  #AnimalCrossing: New Horizons free Summer Update – Wave 1, arriving 7/3! Put on your wet suit to dive & swim in the ocean, and even meet new characters! Stay tuned for info on Wave 2, planned for release in early August.  pic.twitter.com/cYd86R7g6D
 The game will also add another new character, Pascal, a sea otter who you can hit up for new recipes. Anyone who’s played past Animal Crossing titles will recognize Pascal as a chill guy who doles out equally chill pearls of wisdom while casually treading water.
 For a game that revolves around familiar cycles—collecting fruit, pulling weeds, shaking trees to find nice living room furniture—the addition of swimming and diving is actually a pretty big change. And it’s probably a good reason for anyone who went hard on New Horizons in the early days of the pandemic and ran out of things to do to revisit the game. It’ll be interesting to see what else Nintendo has in store New Horizons, since it’s the first Animal Crossing title in in a gaming era that expects of plenty of post-release downloadable content already plotted out on the roadmap.
 It’s also the perfect time to casually stroll out among your villagers while acting like no time passed at all if, like me, your wife accidentally broke one of your Switch controllers and you haven’t played in three weeks. Lolly, if you’re reading this, I want you to know they were back-ordered and this doesn’t change anything between us. Really.
title: 动物穿越的夏季更新将让你为海洋生物游泳
title_en: Animal Crossing’s summer update will let you swim for sea critters

note:
JPL的Pulse Wearable使用3D打印部件和现成的、价格实惠的电子元件，只做一件事：提醒人们不要触摸他们的脸。喷气推进实验室的设计者声称，它非常简单，“任何人都可以很容易地复制这个小玩意儿，无论他们的专业水平如何”，为了鼓励更多的人和公司真正这样做，实验室通过开放源码许可证提供了完整的零部件清单、3D建模文件和完整的组装说明。
脉搏本质上是一个吊坠，戴在离头部6英寸到1英尺的地方绕着脖子，它可以使用基于GaN IR的接近传感器检测到一个人的手何时接近他们的脸。然后，振动马达发出警报，当你的手靠近你的脸时，反应会变得更强。
硬件本身很简单，但这就是关键所在。它的设计使用现成的3V硬币电池，如果你有一台3D打印机可以用来装箱子，还可以访问亚马逊，你可能很快就可以在家里自己组装一台。
PULSE的目标显然不是单枪匹马消灭新冠肺炎-从受污染的手到人的嘴、鼻子或眼睛的接触性传播只是一个媒介，而且似乎很可能导致空气传播的呼吸道飞沫在传播病毒方面至少同样有效。但是，就像经常戴口罩可以极大地降低传播风险一样，最大限度地减少你触摸面部的频率可以与其他减少传播的措施产生很大的组合效应。
实际上，其他健康可穿戴设备可能会在你出现明显症状或检测结果呈阳性之前告诉你新冠肺炎是什么时候患上的-但还需要做一些工作来了解这一功能有多好，以及如何用它来限制暴露。喷气推进实验室的“脉搏”有一个优势，那就是现在在建立积极的习惯方面是有效的，我们知道这些习惯将限制新冠肺炎的传播，以及其他病毒感染。
note_en:
JPL’s PULSE wearable uses 3D-printed parts and readily available, affordable electronic components to do just one thing: remind a person not to touch their face. JPL’s designers claim that its simple enough that the gadget “can easily be reproduced by anyone regardless of their level of expertise,” and to encourage more people and companies to actually do that, the lab has made available a full list of parts, 3D modelling files and full instructions for its assembly via an open source license.
 The PULSE is essentially a pendant, worn between six inches and 1 foot from the head around the neck, which can detect when a person’s hand is approaching their face using gan IR-based proximity sensor. A vibration motor then shakes out an alert, and the response becomes stronger as your hand gets closer to your face.
  The hardware itself is simple – but that’s the point. It’s designed to run on readily available 3V coin batteries, and if you have a 3D printer to hand for the case and access to Amazon, you can probably put one together yourself at home in no time.
 The goal of PULSE obviously isn’t to single-handedly eliminate COVID-19 – contact transmission from contaminated hands to a person’s mouth, nose or eyes is just one vector, and it seems likely that respiratory droplets that result in airborne transmission is at least as effective at passing’s the virus around. But just like regular mask-wearing can dramatically reduce transmission risk, minimizing how often you touch your face can have a big combinatory effect with other measures taken to reduce the spread.
 Other health wearables might actually be able to tell you when you have COVID-19 before you show significant symptoms or have a positive test result – but work still needs to be done to understand how well that works, and how it could be used to limit exposure. JPL’s Pulse has the advantage of being effective now in terms of building positive habits that we know will limit the spread of COVID-19, as well as other viral infections.
title: 美国国家航空航天局喷气推进实验室开源一款反碰脸穿戴设备有助于减少新冠肺炎的传播
title_en: NASA’s JPL open-sources an anti-face touching wearable to help reduce the spread of COVID-19

note:
华特迪士尼公司仍然致力于我们的儿童频道业务，并继续在许多也有迪士尼+的市场执行迪士尼频道的分销协议。
今年秋天，华特迪士尼公司(Walt Disney Co.)将关闭其在英国的三个线性频道，并将内容转移到流媒体服务迪士尼+(Disney+)。
迪士尼公司周四表示，从10月1日起，迪士尼+将成为迪士尼频道、迪士尼XD和迪士尼少年频道内容的英国独家总部。
迪士尼表示，这项直接面向消费者的服务在头七个月就在全球获得了超过5450万订户，现在将首播迪士尼三个频道的所有最新电影、连续剧和特辑，同时还将在英国提供丰富而庞大的迪士尼频道电影目录，包括米老鼠俱乐部、后裔3和菲尼亚斯与费布。
这家好莱坞企业集团补充说：华特迪士尼公司仍然致力于我们的儿童频道业务，并继续在许多也有迪士尼+的市场执行迪士尼频道的分销协议，目标是给我们的粉丝提供多个讲故事的切入点。 &#34；
康卡斯特旗下的付费电视巨头天空英国公司(Sky U.K.)今年早些时候与迪士尼+达成了分销协议，该公司的一名代表表示：除了我们出色的线性频道(来自我们的合作伙伴维亚康姆(Viacom)和特纳(Turner))，我们还有5000多集点播内容，我们也在投资自己的天空儿童原创频道，比如Moominvley和Morph。孩子们可以观看最喜欢的乐高玩具，如Peppa Pig和Paw Patrol，海绵宝宝方裤和口香糖的神奇世界，所有精彩的乐高内容等等。而且，在[付费电视服务]Sky Q.&#34上，你可以在一个地方(连同最好的应用程序，如迪士尼+和Netflix)获得所有这些服务。
一位分析师表示，迪士尼及其在英国的分销合作伙伴越来越难为该公司的三个孩子电视网达成对双方都有财务意义的运输协议，这使得整合频道内容成为合乎逻辑的下一步行动。
PP Foresight分析师保罗·佩斯卡托雷称英国新闻是一个里程碑式的时刻，他解释说：流媒体革命正在加速，不容忽视。公司不能再继续支持一系列渠道，蚕食收入流。未来完全是流媒体，这一最新举措为其他公司树立了效仿的基准。
title: 迪士尼儿童频道在英国关闭，内容转移到迪士尼+
title_en: Disney Kids Channels To Shutter In UK, Content Moving To Disney+

note:
本周，三名北卡罗来纳州警察被解雇，因为调查人员发现警察自己的仪表盘摄像头意外记录下了令人难以置信的种族主义、令人不安的对话和暴力威胁。
威尔明顿警察局长唐尼·威廉姆斯(Donny Williams)周三表示，凯文·皮纳(Kevin Piner)警官巡逻车上的仪表盘摄像头从两个小时的录音中捕捉到了超过46分钟的相关镜头。
根据该部门的报告(PDF)，皮纳的相机是由于意外激活而被记录下来的。那次激活最终捕捉到了皮纳和另外两名警官杰西·摩尔(Jessie Moore)和布莱恩·吉尔摩(Brian Gilmore)--都是白人--使用众所周知的种族主义诽谤，讨论警察中的黑人成员和当地抗议者。摩尔一度说，一名当地治安官，一名黑人妇女，在三人谈论他们对内战即将到来的感受之前，需要一颗子弹击中她的头部，三人都声称已经准备好了。我们只是要出去开始屠杀他们，他妈的-s，&#34；皮纳补充道。
威廉姆斯说，这些谈话非常无礼，理应立即采取行动。当我第一次得知这些对话时，我感到震惊、悲伤和厌恶。在我们的机构和我们的城市里，这种行为是没有立足之地的，也是不能容忍的。
威廉姆斯还补充说，他正在向法院请愿，根据北卡罗来纳州的法律，决定是否可以公开发布这段视频。
警察身体或仪表盘安装的摄像头经常被宣传为警方问责的福音，威尔明顿案件似乎证明了原因。
最近几周，许多州和地方政府争相通过决议，呼吁增加摄像头的使用或资金，以回应正在进行的反对警察暴行和支持黑人社区的全国性抗议运动。例如，在纽约州，州议会刚刚通过立法，要求所有州警察都要配备随身相机。议长卡尔·希斯蒂谈到这项措施时说，作为美国最大的州警察机构之一，纽约州警察局应该是第一批树立榜样的机构之一，向其他人展示如何正确使用随身摄像头，向公众传递透明度和问责性。
有时镜头确实能达到预期效果。例如，2017年，马里兰州有34起刑事案件被驳回，因为穿戴在身上的摄像头拍摄的画面显示，警察在嫌疑人附近种植毒品。
但这项技术本身并不是灵丹妙药。“纽约时报”(New York Times)早在2017年就报道过，大大小小的警察部门都在推出昂贵的随身相机项目，但对这些问题没有一致的答案，或者根据政策专家的说法，没有令人信服的证据表明摄像头确保了公众要求的问责程度，而这些问题似乎还没有答案。
2017年在华盛顿特区进行的一项研究发现，自从首都的警察开始佩戴随身相机后，警察的行为或公民投诉没有改变。2019年，研究人员发表了一项对70项关于警察随身相机的研究的荟萃分析，发现DC并不是唯一的一个，而且相机对警察行为或投诉的整体影响并不是始终如一的或显著的。
隐私权倡导者也一直在警告随身携带的警用摄像头带来的危险，不仅是当警方与应该享有隐私的个人交谈时，而且在示威者认为自己是匿名的大规模抗议活动等情况下也是如此。
美国公民自由联盟(American Civil Liberties Union)今天在一篇博客文章中写道，改革积极分子不应该简单地要求硬件。他们应该要求有效的人体相机项目，这些项目不仅包括相机，还包括强有力的政策和制度实践，以使这些相机有效。
note_en:
Three North Carolina police officers were fired from their jobs this week after investigators found incredibly racist, troubling conversations, and threats of violence accidentally recorded by the cops' own dashboard camera.
 The dashboard camera in Officer Kevin Piner's patrol car captured more than 46 minutes of relevant footage from a two-hour recording, Wilmington Police Chief Donny Williams  said Wednesday.
 According to the department's report ( PDF), Piner's camera was recording due to "accidental activation." That activation ended up capturing Piner and two other officers, Jessie Moore and Brian Gilmore—all white—discussing Black members of the force as well as local protesters, using well-known racist slurs. At one point, Moore said a local magistrate, a Black woman, "needed a bullet in her head," before the three discussed their feelings that a civil war was coming, for which all three claimed to be ready. "We are just gonna go out and start slaughtering them fucking n------s," Piner added.
 The conversations "were brutally offensive and deserved immediate action," Williams said. "When I first learned of these conversations, I was shocked, saddened, and disgusted. There is no place for this behavior in our agency or our city and it will not be tolerated."
 Williams also added that he was petitioning the court to determine whether the footage could be released publicly, in accordance with  North Carolina law.
  Police body or dashboard-mounted cameras are  often promoted as a boon to police accountability, and the Wilmington case seems to prove why.
 Many state and local governments have rushed to adopt resolutions in recent weeks calling for increased camera usage or funding, in response the ongoing nationwide protest movement against police brutality and in support of Black communities. In New York state, for example, the state Assembly  just passed legislation requiring body cameras for all state police. Speaker Carl Heastie said about the measure, "As one of the largest state police agencies in the country, the New York State Police should be one of the first agencies to set an example, to show others how to properly use body cams to deliver transparency and accountability to the public."
 Sometimes the footage does indeed work as intended. In 2017, for example, 34 criminal cases were  thrown out in Maryland after footage from body-worn cameras showed police planting drugs near suspects.
 But the technology is not in and of itself a panacea. "Police departments large and small are rolling out expensive body-camera programs without consistent answers to the questions or, according to policy experts, convincing evidence that the cameras ensure the level of accountability that the public demands,"  The New York Times reported back in 2017, and those questions do not yet seem to have answers.
 A study conducted in Washington, DC, in 2017 found  no change in police behavior or citizen complaints after police in the nation's capital began wearing body cameras. In 2019, researchers who  published a meta-analysis of 70 studies on police body-worn cameras found that DC was not alone, and cameras did not have "a consistent or significant effect" on officer behavior or complaints overall.
  Privacy advocates have also warned consistently about the dangers posed by body-worn police cameras, not only when police are  speaking with individuals who deserve privacy, but also in situations such as  mass protests when demonstrators believe they have anonymity.
 "Reform activists should not simply demand hardware," the American Civil Liberties Union wrote in  a blog post today. "They should demand effective body camera  programs, which include not just cameras but also strong policies and institutional practices to make those cameras effective."
title: 三名警察在意外激活仪表盘摄像头捕捉到种族主义咆哮后被解雇
title_en: Three cops fired after accidental dashcam activation captures racist rants
